item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! 072-5 does suffer slightly from poorly placed expunging, though. I always find it irritating when authors decide to introduce the concept of a more interesting consequence of an SCP's effect and decide to censor only that, as if you're just supposed to imagine something interesting is written there. As it is, the sleeping bag may as well be the same as any other bed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would just like to note that ever since I read this article I have been unable to sleep without both feet being completely underneath and tucked into my blanket. I hope you're proud of yourself >:(

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
T H E F O O T T H I E F
Anywho, this SCP is really cool! Certainly deserves more than 471 upvotes.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit to this article was reverted, as "I believe that this the more scientific representation" is not a strong enough reason to justify this edit.

In the future, please contact staff about stylistic edits on other authors' articles before implementing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My fear of leaving a foot out has worsened after reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the first SCPs i ever read. Freaked the hell out of me back then..

Good Job +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, not so scary for me. I live with a little monster who constantly tries to maul my feet, even if they ARE covered.

She's a kitten, and full of murder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see some expansion on this one. Experiment logs would be revealing. Has the Foundation been able to determine where the flesh does go, or how long it takes for new instances to infect beds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How dare you manifest my childhood fears that Satan will eat my feet into an SCP!
Regardless, I'm probably not sleeping tonight.

item.title: I already had insomnia I swear
item.content:encoded: Fuck this one, seriously fuck it. I already have trouble sleeping and now I've git to worry about this dark recess of my imagination. It was a creepy as hell read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually the first SCP I have ever read, I decided to come back to it and upvote it.

Remember, any SCP could be someone's first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember quite a few sleepless nights after reading this one. Well done.

+1

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Check out the creep factor!

https://youtu.be/KS0DTFp6DEQ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had trouble sleeping with my feet sticking out from under my covers for MONTHS after reading this.

A+ job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading another scp reference this and that it isn't what it seems…anyone know what I'm talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its a bit late to be posting this but 5 lux is more than enough lighting to properly record even a translucent shadowy creature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can I change it like…

No, unless you have permission from the original author or a staff member.

I think eating your foot is a bit more then safe….

"Safe class" does not mean "not dangerous". Object classes are based primarily on how difficult something is to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it is called Safe primarily due to this:

Instances of SCP-072 have only been observed to manifest when a human (hereafter referred to as “the subject”) enters REM sleep while located in a bed ‘infected’ by SCP-072 and leaves a foot or feet exposed to open air.

Ergo, the SCP does not activate its anomalous effect unless someone does something to it. When you do not sleep on it, nothing happens.

But Object Classes are usually the author's choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this safe?!?!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this SCP safe?!?!!? Can I change it like… I think eating your foot is a bit more then safe….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great, an SCP with a foot fetish…..

item.title: Having constant sleep paralysis makes this SCp better
item.content:encoded: Yes I get Horrible sleep paralysis , I actually spend a few hours unable move seeing figures at the egde of my eyesight . Its pretty weird becuase sometimes I feel something scratching at me . ….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, very creepy to me. One of my favorites SCPs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always hated leaving my feet uncovered but I thought it was a comfort thing and apparently its a legit fear? I mean I'm not surprised because again I can't sleep well with legs sticking out, but, huh. Huuuuh.

Anyway, I like this +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…and why haven't I +1'd this already? Let's fix that. There y'go.

My favorite part of this is how the hand will specifically wake you up first, tapping gently. The paralysis and mutilation I feel is… and I don't want this to sound like a criticism, but "obvious". Without them there'd just be no horror at all. You need them there to have an article.

But the polite tapping… that just reeks of pure spite and malice and takes this to a whole other level. Just made me shiver and it's 10:52 AM in bright sunny Florida.

Edit: Was listening to the Kaktuskast podcast episode with Kate as a guest, pausing and reading links as I went. I did not realize Kate also lives in Florida. Did not intend to be creepy, just meant "it's broad daylight and this made me shiver in fear."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright so some people say this to be funny, some say it to give the review more meaning.

Well, when I am saying "I can't sleep," it is because I literally cannot sleep. I might sound weird but this is my absolute biggest fear (something under bed getting exposed feet or other body part). If there is a name for this fear like arachnophobia or claustrophobia, I have it. And my god, Kate you have literally horrified me.
Sometimes when I read horror stories, creepypastas, or watch scary movies and such, I always say to myself "oh man I won't be able to sleep." Even so I drift off in bed in the wee hours of the night, but Kate, you made that little statement of mine true.

It may not make a few here even interested, but this being a real fear of mine, and given actual detail instead of a small figment of my imagination, has figuratively made me shit my pants. I've been awake for… I don't know how long it has been since 10 A.M. yesterday, but I've been up longer than 24 hours. All because of you.

10 out of terrifying 10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much as zygomatic asked, I am curious as to alternate sleep positions and what happens. If the subject sleeps across the bed with feet hanging off, do the hands come up the sides? (more applicable for Double and larger beds, but enquiring minds and all that rot).

Otherwise…wow. Creeped out. Glad I read this at work in the daylight. I've never had a specific fear of foot assault by the MUB (Monster Under the Bed), but inexplicable mutilation seriously gives me the heebies…+1 many times over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could upvote this again, I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first read this aritlce, I agreed with most of what users such as Yoric had said. But after reading it again, I now remember how I'd frequently suffered from sleep paralysis as a kid. That shit is terrifying, and I am seriously shaking just thinking about some thing mutiliating me while I lay there, unable to move. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this SCP is the reason I can't sleep comfortably in my bed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has a foot fetish…..
Ew, sick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That could me interesting, but could up it to Keter. This Really dose not need to be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be classified as Euclid for what it can do to a Humanoid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens when someone sleeps with their head at the foot of the bed…? Oh sweet Jesus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And this is why I always roll my feet in the blanket like a burrito …

Also, yay +100!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first one I ever read, and I was instantly hooked. It really makes me think about a toe sticking out at night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The addendum states that 072-2 is a twin-sized bed, but the one in the picture clearly isn't.

Downvoted because this relies entirely on a specific fear, which I not only don't have, but didn't even know before reading this that it was something people could be scared of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question to the author (If he/she's still here): Were you inspired to write this as a result of a bed bug infestation? I have one right now, and I find interesting parallels between my feet being attacked by the damn things, and my constant need to scratch feeling like I'm scraping my skin off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was the sleeping bag that pushed it over the top for me to vote +1; perfect use of redaction there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So losing a foot is not fatal? I understand that you can treat that, but by the time this thing is done cutting away your foot, how much blood have you already lost at that point?

Interesting nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YEESH, this one gave me a serious case of the heebie-jeebies. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, after this I'll be certain to cover myself well tonight.
There was only one part that confused me. When it's taking off the flesh from people's feet, how much is it taking? The whole foot, or just chunks of it? While reading this, I pictured it amputating the foot. Though I suspect that image wasn't the intention.

Nevertheless, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Call Patton Oswalt, because you made a convincingly scary version of Death Bed. +1

Also it was really stupid of me to search the "sleep" tag while in bed with the lights out. Holy crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-072-5, a sleeping bag with bottom removed introduced to SCP-072-1 for testing. When D-2191 entered REM sleep in object, SCP-999-J manifested within the sleeping bag, wiped Cheeto dust on D-2191's Foundation-issue Batman pajamas, discussed Kate Upton's recent photo shoot, giggled in a manner that caused D-2191 great discomfort, and demanifested. SCP-072-5 not recommended for testing in future.

Wanna bet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-072-5, a sleeping bag with bottom removed introduced to SCP-072-1 for testing. When D-2191 entered REM sleep in object, [DATA EXPUNGED]. SCP-072-5 not recommended for testing in future.

One of the few sorts of expungement that I rather enjoy… There's not really anything that can be inferred here other than horror-terror.

Upvoted, with a side of making sure my bed is well made tonight. >.<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WHY? WHY?! IT'S 10PM HERE IN PERTH AND YOU EXPECT ME TO BE ABLE TO SLEEP NOW?!

In normal words though, fuck. This shit gets me scared. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck you. Insomnia, here I come.

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is precisely the reason I wear socks to bed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this would be better if, after being tapped, the disembodied voice of a polite butler would state "Pardon me sir, but it appears that you have improperly placed your covers, you might want to correct them before you catch a cold."

Because nothing is scarier than British butlers watching you whilst you slumber….

(Completely disregard everything you just read.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written, and preys on a fairly acute fear: you either don't have it, or OH GOD WHY. I'm wearing combat boots to bed tonight, you can be sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EDWARD SCISSORHANDS AND FREDDY KRUGER HAVE A CANNIBALISTIC FOOT FETISH AND LIVE UNDER YOUR BED.
On a serious note, this is pretty good. I read this over a month ago when I was a lurker, and it still haunts me. I will never sleep with my feet exposed, regardless of circumstances. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamnit, I thought this was going to be a nice simple paralysis fear thing.

Then it had to go and slice off people's feet. Fucking hell. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I STILL sleep with my feet under a cover. No matter what. This is my fear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Asghasgahgshas FUUUUUUCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, not trying to be a dick or force my changes on anyone. I honestly interpreted the rules of that section and the author's post as "please work on my article, you have my permission".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Regardless. The author asked for suggestions, not for it to be rewritten. Don't be a dick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think my revision was that big of a change to be called a rewrite or overhaul. The only section that I largely changed was the containment procedures, which was what the article needed to have improved. The author didn't ask for just suggestions on how to improve it, they asked for it to be improved: "as long as it's there, I'll like to see it improve".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please note that the author, in their post on that thread, asked for suggestions on how to improve it and not a rewrite or overhaul.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might have been a good idea to mention that in your post…
